channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Pearl Abyss Explains Crimson Desert’s Player-First Updates
item.content:encoded:
Pearl Abyss, the makers of Crimson Desert, are now speaking on the game’s success and discussing their approach to player feedback and how they update the game accordingly.

Crimson Desert has been a historic game for the South Korean entertainment industry. The game has recently surpassed 5 million sales, and Pearl Abyss was even commended by South Korean Prime Minister Kim Min-seok for the accomplishment. Now, a company executive is discussing some of the details behind the game’s development.

Pearl Abyss Details Crimson Desert’s Live Feedback Approach

Pearl Abyss’s head of publishing, Will Powers, recently spoke with The Washington Post about the game’s success. One of the main topics of discussion was the team’s responsiveness and the regular updates delivered outside a pre-announced roadmap. Powers says it was intentional not to have a roadmap. They didn’t want set-in-stone dates to answer to. More importantly, they didn’t want to make any presumptions.

“Everything, patch-wise, content-wise, has been iterated in real time based on feedback, based on response,” Powers said. “If you bake in a road map, you’re presuming. We are not baking in presumptions around what the players want,” he added.

However, they also respond to fans’ innovativeness and the tricks they find to make the game a little easier. For example, some players discovered a method to make Kliff move quickly in the air with a stabbing technique. That wasn’t intentional, but rather than patching out the ability, they added an animation to the maneuver and gave it a little flair.

“We’re not onerous about it — if an idea didn’t come from us, then it can’t be in the game,” Powers said. “I think that’s something that [other companies are] too ego-driven a lot of the time to be able to accept other people’s ideas. It’s almost Silicon Valley-esque. A good idea can come from anywhere.”

For more information on Crimson Desert, visit Insider Gaming to see a preview of the upcoming features and content shared by the developers.

How do you feel about Crimson Desert so far? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: PlayStation Store’s Deals Tab Has Disappeared
item.content:encoded:
For the first time in a very long time, the ‘Deals’ tab on the PlayStation Store has gone missing.

For those who don’t know, Sony is currently facing a class action lawsuit that would require it to pay about $7.8 million to all U.S. residents who purchased specific digital games through the PlayStation Network. Since then, the first new development for the store has arrived, and it’s one that some may never have seen before.

PlayStation Store Doesn’t Have a Deals Tab Right Now

Spotted by PushSquare, if you head over to the PlayStation Store on your console, in the mobile app, or on the website, you will see that the Deals tab is no longer there. The Deals tab has always been filled with discounts for PS4 and PS5 games, no matter how minuscule, and it’s just strange to not see that tab anymore. A sale was planned for today, May 7; however, it seems Sony may have decided against it.

The report also talks about another reason that could have resulted in the ‘Deals’ tabs disappearing. PlayStation has been working on UI changes for its current-gen console, which could mean it is also reworking the PlayStation Store. However, that change would’ve appeared in a beta version rather than the standard version, so it might be that the store doesn’t have any active deals at the moment.

In other news, here’s how you can check eligibility and claim money from the recent Sony PlayStation lawsuit. Additionally, the PlayStation Plus free monthly games for May 2026 have been revealed, and they include a very popular sports title. What are your thoughts on the PlayStation Store’s ‘Deals’ tab disappearing? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the patch notes for the GTA Online Nightclub Bonuses update. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
